testcase.name: prior_normal_vector_works_as_expected
testcase.system-out: Non-empty input resulted in an empty StanModule object. Is the input correct? Backtrace: ▆ 1. ├─testthat::expect_equal(...) at test-Prior.R:287:5 2. │ └─testthat::quasi_label(enquo(expected), expected.label) 3. │ └─rlang::eval_bare(expr, quo_get_env(quo)) 4. └─jmpost::StanModule(test_path("models", "Prior_4.stan"))

testcase.name: prior_normal_vector_works_as_expected
testcase.failure:
Expected `x_stan_module` to equal `StanModule(test_path("models", "Prior_4.stan"))`. Differences: actual@data vs expected@data - " int<lower=1> prior_dim_mus_bob;" - " int<lower=1> prior_dim_sigmas_bob;" - " vector[prior_dim_mus_bob] prior_mus_bob;" - " vector<lower=0>[prior_dim_sigmas_bob] prior_sigmas_bob;" + ""
`actual@model`: " bob ~ normal(prior_mus_bob, prior_sigmas_bob);" `expected@model`: ""

testcase.name: Different_priors_for_the_beta_components_are_possible
testcase.failure:
Adding new snapshot: Code x <- SurvivalExponential(beta = prior_normal(0, 1)) print(x) Output
Exponential Survival Model with parameters: sm_exp_lambda ~ gamma(alpha = 2, beta = 5) beta_os_cov ~ normal(mu = 0, sigma = 1)

testcase.name: Different_priors_for_the_beta_components_are_possible
testcase.failure:
Adding new snapshot: Code x <- SurvivalExponential(beta = prior_normal_vector(c(0, 1, 2), c(1, 2, 3))) print(x) Output
Exponential Survival Model with parameters: sm_exp_lambda ~ gamma(alpha = 2, beta = 5) beta_os_cov ~ normal(mus = [0, 1, 2], sigmas = [1, 2, 3])

testcase.name: Longitudinal_Model_doesn_t_print_sampler_rejection_messages
testcase.failure: Expected `any(...)` to be FALSE. Differences: `actual`: TRUE `expected`: FALSE
